title: Wholemeal cheese scones
subtitle: Savoury scones of wholemeal flour and mature cheddar
excerpt: Wholemeal cheese scones are a savoury scone made with half wholemeal and half plain flour, with a generous amount of mature cheddar in the dough and more on top. The wholemeal flour gives them a slightly nutty, denser texture than a white scone; the cheese provides enough salt that they need none added. Good with butter and nothing else.
sourceNotes: Cheese scones are a well-established variant of the British scone, documented in British home baking through the twentieth century. The wholemeal version is a simple adaptation. The formula here follows standard British scone method adapted for wholemeal flour and cheddar.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked; reheat from frozen in a 160°C oven for 12 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best on the day, still warm.

content.text: Wholemeal cheese scones are a savoury scone made with a mix of wholemeal and plain flour, matured cheddar throughout, and a topping of grated cheese that forms a crisp, slightly burnt-edged crust as it bakes. The wholemeal adds weight and a faint nuttiness; the cheese provides salt and depth. No added salt is needed if the cheese is mature enough.

items.prepNote: finely grated; 120 g for the dough, 30 g for the top

content.text: Sift both flours and the baking powder into a large bowl; tip in any bran left in the sieve. Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Stir in 120 g of the grated cheese, the mustard powder, and cayenne. Add most of the buttermilk and bring together in 8 to 10 strokes with a palette knife. Pat the dough on a floured surface to 3 cm thick and cut rounds with a 5 cm cutter pressed straight down.

Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Place the scones on a lined baking tray. Brush the tops with beaten egg and scatter the reserved 30 g of cheese on each. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes until risen, golden brown, and the cheese on top has melted and coloured at the edges. Cool briefly on a wire rack; eat warm.
